name: Radxa ZERO 3W
description: RK3566 in Pi Zero form factor

status.comment: Still listed on radxa.com and stocked by major distributors as of 2026-04.

open-source-hardware.comment: Radxa publishes a schematic PDF (v1.11) but no PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license; per RESEARCH.md guideline, schematics-only does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Radxa (Shenzhen Radxa Computer Co., Ltd.) is headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: RK3566 SoC is rated up to 1.8 GHz, but Radxa specifies the ZERO 3W at 1.6 GHz max.

process-node.comment: RK3566 is fabricated on a 22 nm process; the specific foundry (Samsung is widely cited but unconfirmed) is not documented on Radxa's board page.

vulkan-support.comment: Mali-G52 2EE supports Vulkan 1.1 per vendor documentation.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Rockchip's RK3566 datasheet quotes 1.0 TOPS @ INT8; Radxa's launch coverage on Hackster cited 0.8 TOPS for the Zero 3W. Used Rockchip's higher SoC-level figure but the discrepancy is real.

hw-video-decode.comment: RK3566 video unit decodes H.264/H.265/VP9 up to 4K@60; no AV1 hardware decode.

hw-video-encode.comment: H.264/H.265 encode up to 1080p60 per Radxa product page.

emmc.comment: Optional 8/16/32/64 GB eMMC 5.1; sold separately by configuration.

ethernet.comment: ZERO 3W is the wireless variant — Ethernet is on the sister ZERO 3E.

wifi.comment: Radxa product page advertises 'Wi-Fi 6 / BT 5.4'. Initial Oct 2023 announcement on Hackster mentioned only Wi-Fi 4/BT 5.0; shipping units use Radxa Wireless Module D8 with Wi-Fi 6 + BT 5.4.

bluetooth.comment: Shipping units have Bluetooth 5.4 per Radxa and Liliputing; older Radxa docs page still says BT 5.0 — go by the product page and confirmed shipping silicon.

usb-a-3x-ports.comment: USB 3.0 is exposed only on a USB-C connector, not USB-A.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 3.0 host (Type-C) + 1× USB 2.0 OTG (Type-C, also accepts power).

poe-support.comment: No Ethernet, so no PoE path.

max-display-resolution.comment: Output capped at 1080p60 over micro-HDMI per Radxa product page; the SoC can decode 4K but display output is 1080p.

displayport.comment: No DisplayPort output (USB-C ports do not advertise DP-Alt mode).

mipi-dsi.comment: Only a MIPI-CSI camera connector is present; no MIPI-DSI display interface.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm jack on the board; audio over HDMI.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 40-pin header with Raspberry Pi-compatible pinout; sold optionally pre-soldered.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Pi Zero 2 W form factor (65×30 mm) is too small for full-size HATs; while the GPIO header is electrically RPi-compatible, mechanical HAT mounting and HAT EEPROM auto-config are not provided.

pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: RK3566 has a PCIe 2.1 x1 controller but it is not routed out on the ZERO 3W.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I²C, SPI, and UART are exposed on the 40-pin header.

idle-power-w.comment: No credible measured idle figure published by Radxa or major reviewers; per RESEARCH.md, prefer null over guessing from TDP.

load-power-w.comment: No credible measured load figure published; reviews note thermals warrant a heatsink but do not quote sustained wattage.

cooling-required.comment: Vendor offers an official heatsink (5519A) and community testing on the Radxa forum reports thermal throttling under sustained load without one.

rtc-battery-header.comment: No RTC battery header on the PCB.

form-factor-class.comment: 65×30 mm matches the Raspberry Pi Zero family footprint.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Mounting holes follow Pi Zero pattern, but connector positions (micro-HDMI vs mini-HDMI, USB-C vs micro-USB) mean Pi Zero cases generally don't fit without modification.

weight-g.comment: Bare-board weight not published by Radxa or major reviewers.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Armbian lists the board as Stable on the current 6.18.x mainline branch; mainline U-Boot 2024.10+ supports it. RK3566 has been mainline since ~5.16 with steadily growing peripheral coverage.

official-os-image.comment: Radxa publishes officially-tested Debian images (bookworm KDE on 6.1 kernel, bullseye XFCE on 5.10).

distros-with-images.comment: Official Radxa Debian images; Ubuntu (24.04 minimal/desktop) and Debian rolling images via Armbian; DietPi adds a community image.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot (mainline 2024.10+ as of recent Armbian); Rockchip's idbloader/SPL chain in front of U-Boot proper.

long-term-support.comment: Radxa does not publish a stated software-support window for the ZERO series; community/Armbian backstop is the practical longevity story.

android-support.comment: Radxa publishes an official Android 11 image (Radxa-Zero3WE-20231130-gpt.zip) covering both ZERO 3W and 3E.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Radxa launch MSRP: $15 for 1 GB RAM / no eMMC / no header. Retailers (Allnet, Arace, Evelta, Ameridroid) typically charge $18–$22 for the same SKU as of 2026-04.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Radxa launch top-tier MSRP: $65 for 8 GB RAM + 64 GB eMMC. Retailers commonly mark this up to ~$70+.

available-regions.comment: Allnet (CN/EU), Ameridroid (US), Arace (US), Evelta (India) all stock it — broad global retail coverage.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at major distributors as of 2026-04-26 — Arace and Allnet show stock for most SKUs (some 8 GB configs intermittently sold out).

documentation-quality.comment: Solid official docs portal (getting-started, hardware, OS install, schematics PDF). Falls short of a 5 because PCB layout files and detailed mechanical drawings aren't published.

community-size.comment: Active Radxa forum and Armbian sub-thread; Radxa is an established second-tier vendor per RESEARCH.md guideline.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum with daily activity in the Zero Series subforum.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official heatsink (5519A) and antenna kit; no official case or display. Pi Zero cases mostly do not fit. Generic 40-pin GPIO accessories work mechanically subject to the smaller footprint.
